title: Workshop overview
text:
Location and dates

San Cristóbal Island, Galápagos
June 8 to June 12, 2026

title: Workshop overview
text:
Workshop guidelines and confidentiality
We aim for this workshop to be a space for open and transparent discussion. All conversations will follow the Chatham House Rule. If you choose to use social media, we ask that you remain mindful of the sensitivity of the topics discussed and be selective about what you share.
If you are presenting and prefer that your material is not shared on social media, please make this clear at the beginning of your presentation or in your slides.






CautionChatham House Rule





“When a meeting, or part of it, is held under the Chatham House Rule, participants are free to use the information received, but neither the identity nor the affiliation of the speaker, nor that of any other participant, may be revealed.”

title: Workshop overview
text:
Acknowledgements
The Moore Center for Science and Solutions of Conservation International acknowledges the support of the German Government through the IKI program, as well as the consortium partners involved in the Save the Blue Five project (CPPS, GIZ, and MarViva) for making this workshop possible.

title: Workshop logistics
text:
Location
San Cristóbal Island, Galápagos

title: Workshop logistics
text:
Dates
June 8 to June 12, 2026

title: Workshop logistics
text:
Venue
The workshop will be hosted at Universidad San Francisco de Quito (USFQ) - San Cristóbal Campus.
The main working space is currently planned to be Classroom 2, which includes a screen, air conditioning, student desks, and a whiteboard. Additional venue instructions and access details will be shared closer to the workshop date.

title: Workshop logistics
text:
Travel note
Participants may need to stay overnight on mainland Ecuador on June 7, 2026 before flying to San Cristóbal on June 8, 2026, depending on their travel itinerary.
Additional travel instructions, hotel details, and local transportation information will be shared by email.

title: Day 3 - From Models to Decisions: Bridging Science and Regional Needs
text:
Objective
Translate scientific and modeling progress into concrete next steps, aligning technical development with regional priorities and decision-making needs.

title: Day 4 - Applied Science and Synthesis
text:
Objective
Bring together the main outcomes from the workshop, translate them into concrete outputs, and define a clear path forward for collaboration and regional impact.

title: Day 2 - Scientific Progress and Modeling Integration
text:
Objective
Consolidate scientific and modeling progress since the 2025 workshop, assess readiness for climate projections, and identify key gaps to advance the project.
crumbs: Day 2 - Scientific Progress and Modeling Integration
